summary: Large warning-cleanup PR that made Clang-style analysis stricter by fixing many implicit function declarations across Dolphin SDK, Runtime, fighter, item, stage, and project support files. The main tactic was to add missing headers/prototypes, normalize types and callback signatures, introduce small per-module headers, and gate CodeWarrior/Gekko-only constructs behind `defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO)`. The PR also tightened `compile_flags.txt` with additional `-Werror` diagnostics and adjusted the Makefile to define `GEKKO` for matching builds.
what_changed: Added or moved many function declarations into headers to eliminate `implicit-function-declaration` warnings, especially in Dolphin CARD/DVD/GX/OS/AX modules and fighter code.

Expanded `compile_flags.txt` so analysis treats additional issues as errors: `incompatible-pointer-types`, `pointer-type-mismatch`, `implicit-int`, `c2x-extensions`, and `missing-field-initializers`; also disabled `unused-but-set-variable` warnings.
Reformatted Makefile `CFLAGS` and added `-DGEKKO`, enabling source to distinguish Gekko/CodeWarrior-only paths.
Introduced `src/MetroTRK/intrinsics.h` for intrinsic declarations such as `__sync`, `__isync`, and `__cntlzw`.

Introduced several small AX headers (`AX.h`, `AXAlloc.h`, `AXCL.h`, `AXInit.h`, `AXOut.h`, `AXSPB.h`, `AXVPB.h`) and included them from `src/dolphin/ax/AX.c` so initialization helpers are declared before use.
Updated `include/dolphin/card.h` to include `OSExi.h` and changed `CARDCallback` from `s32 chan` to `EXIChannel chan`, reducing type ambiguity in CARD callback plumbing.
Moved `memcpy` availability through `include/string.h` by including `Runtime/__mem.h` rather than keeping a duplicate local prototype.
Deleted the old `include/dolphin/os/OSCache.h` and added declarations under the source-side OSCache header path listed in the changed files.
Added OS declarations such as `OSCancelAlarm`, `OSInitAlarm`, `OSCreateAlarm`, `OSRegisterResetFunction`, `__OSDoHotReset`, and `__OSUnhandledException` where callers needed visible prototypes.
Added math/runtime intrinsic declarations including `__frsqrte`, `__HI`, and `__LO` in `include/math.h`.

Changed Runtime declarations in `src/Runtime/__mem.h` from `#ifndef M2CTX` gating to `#if defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO)` around `__declspec(section ".init")`, making compiler-specific syntax conditional.
Wrapped Gekko/CodeWarrior-only assembly and section attributes in `src/Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c`, with a non-Gekko placeholder implementation for `__init_cpp_exceptions`.
Added `src/placeholder.h` with a `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` panic helper, reviewed toward using `__func__` in the panic message.
Added `Vec3` as a preferred alias in `include/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h` while leaving older aliases (`Vec`, `Point3d`, pointer aliases) with TODO notes for future cleanup.
Documented abbreviation glossary additions for `vec` and `vtx`.
Touched many Fox and Donkey fighter files and fighter headers to resolve missing declarations and type issues, with exact symbol-level intent not fully visible in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Turned a broad warning category into a systematic header/prototype cleanup instead of suppressing `implicit-function-declaration` globally.
Added stricter warning errors only after doing cleanup, using compiler diagnostics as a project-wide guardrail for future decomp work.
Used small, module-local headers for Dolphin internals rather than scattering ad-hoc `extern` declarations in C files.
Made `GEKKO` an explicit build define and guarded CodeWarrior/Gekko-only constructs with `defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO)`, improving compatibility with host tooling and analysis.
Chose to include the canonical Runtime memory header from `string.h` rather than maintain duplicate `memcpy` declarations.
Preserved unresolved type/name uncertainty with `@todo` comments, such as vector alias cleanup, callback casts, and placeholder linker naming, instead of pretending the decomp model was final.
Changed `CARDCallback` to use `EXIChannel`, aligning callback parameter types with the subsystem that owns the channel concept.
decomp_lessons: Implicit declaration cleanup often reveals real type-model problems, especially callback parameter types and internal SDK declarations.
Prefer placing declarations in the header that semantically owns the function, even if the implementation lives elsewhere due to current splits.

When a C standard function is implemented in a project-specific Runtime file, expose it through the standard-facing header by including the implementation header rather than duplicating inconsistent prototypes.
Use platform/compiler feature guards for `__declspec`, inline assembly, and other CodeWarrior-specific constructs so non-matching tooling can still parse or compile source.
For decomp placeholders, prefer runtime panic helpers that report the current function via `__func__`, but remember `__func__` is not a preprocessor macro.
Avoid solving every type cast in a warning PR if it would expand scope; leave precise TODOs, but future work should propagate the correct type through function definitions to eliminate casts.
Struct aliases for clone characters may legitimately be shared; review noted that clone character attributes and Nana appear to use the same struct layouts as their originals.
Vector naming should move toward base names like `Vec3` and `Vec4`, with older SDK names retained as aliases until cleanup is safe.
naming_conventions: Header guards were normalized toward descriptive paths such as `DOLPHIN_BASE_PPCARCH_H` instead of older underscore-heavy guards like `_DOLPHIN_PPCARCH_H_`.
Glossary additions establish `vec` as short for vector and `vtx` as short for vertex.
`Vec3` was introduced as the preferred base name for 3-component float vectors, while `Vec`, `Point3d`, and pointer aliases remain temporary aliases.

Review discussion indicates `ftCLinkAttributes` may not need a distinct struct if it is the same as `ftLinkAttributes`; clone character structs can sometimes share original-character attribute layouts.
OS reset constants were parenthesized and grouped with Doxygen comments under an `OS_RESET_CODE` defgroup, with a TODO to convert to an enum.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added `-DGEKKO` in Makefile CFLAGS so matching builds can enable Gekko-specific sections while host analysis can avoid them.
Guarded CodeWarrior section placement (`__declspec(section ".init")`) with `#if defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO)`.
Guarded inline assembly helper `GetR2` in `__init_cpp_exceptions.c` behind the same CodeWarrior/Gekko condition.
Provided a non-Gekko `__init_cpp_exceptions` path that calls `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, allowing non-target builds to type-check without implementing target-specific assembly behavior.
Used intrinsic prototypes in `MetroTRK/intrinsics.h` and `math.h` so compiler builtins/runtime helpers are explicitly declared rather than implicitly assumed.
Review discussed pointer arithmetic in `OSExi.c`: prefer `buf = (u8*) buf + xLen;` over attempting `(u8*) buf += xLen`, and validate whether the construct actually affects generated behavior.
review_feedback: For `src/placeholder.h`, r-burns agreed that using `__func__` in `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` was appropriate, with the caveat that `__func__` is not a preprocessor macro and support detection differs from normal macros.
For `src/placeholder.h`, suggested panic form was `OSPanic(__FILE__, __LINE__, "%s is not implemented!", __func__);`.

For `src/dolphin/card/CARDCreate.c`, r-burns noted that `CARDCallback` casts could be removed by propagating `EXIChannel` to related function definitions; ribbanya acknowledged but considered it out of scope for this PR.
For `src/dolphin/os/OSAlarm.c`, r-burns suggested assigning `alarm->handler = NULL;` instead of a hacky whole-struct or integer cast write.
For `src/dolphin/os/OSExi.c`, r-burns suggested `buf = (u8*) buf + xLen;`; discussion questioned whether the existing cast-assignment compiled and whether it did anything.
For `include/string.h`, r-burns questioned deleting `memcpy`; final direction in the excerpt was that `memcpy` belongs in the C standard header and `string.h` should include `Runtime/__mem.h`.
For `include/dolphin/types.h`, review confirmed the conditional `AT_ADDRESS` macro shape for `PERMUTER`, `__MWERKS__`, and fallback cases.

For `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCLink/ftclink.h`, review asked whether the TODO meant `ftCLinkAttributes` or `ftLinkAttributes`; ribbanya clarified clone characters and Nana appear to share the same struct layouts.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that eliminate `CARDCallback` casts by propagating `EXIChannel` through CARD function definitions.
Search for later cleanup of `OSAlarm` initialization hacks and whether `alarm->handler = NULL` was adopted.
Search for `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` and `__func__` usage to confirm the final placeholder macro form.
Find PRs that complete `Vec3`/`Vec4` naming cleanup and remove pointer aliases from `mtxtypes.h`.
Search for follow-up changes to clone character attribute structs, especially `ftCLinkAttributes`, `ftLinkAttributes`, and Nana.
Audit remaining `implicit-function-declaration` suppressions or warnings after PR 653.
Search for later refactors of Runtime `__mem.h`, `include/string.h`, and standard C library header exposure.
Look for OSExi pointer arithmetic fixes around `EXIImmEx` and `buf = (u8*) buf + xLen`.
